title: Policy evidence portability and order-invariance correction
outcome: Corrected the two residual S5.2 evidence defects found in follow-up review. The nine-case request/child merge matrix now uses distinct layer-specific rule IDs, reverses two genuine rules within each layer, reverses policy-registry order, swaps request/child source order, and asserts byte-identical serialized public decisions plus deterministic primary rules. The Unix permission-denied path test now probes the same metadata boundary used by path normalization and returns only when a privileged runner cannot reproduce PermissionDenied, so root-owned containers no longer fail for retaining traversal through mode 000. Production policy behavior remains unchanged and spawn-free.
validation: PASS in the available compatibility environment: cargo test --ignore-rust-version -p takogami (105 library + 78 integration = 183); focused merge and permission tests; cargo check --ignore-rust-version; Ontarch sync + validate; Panoply Brewfile generation check + substrate validation; bash syntax; JSON parse; git diff --check. Compatibility toolchain was rustc/cargo 1.88.0. Repository-pinned Rust 1.96.0, Moon 2.3.2, rustfmt, and Clippy were unavailable in this review environment and are not claimed as rerun for this follow-up delta.
decisions: Layer-order determinism is evidenced with different request and child rule IDs; identical IDs cannot prove primary-rule tie-breaking.
Rule-order determinism is evidenced by reversing two distinct same-effect command rules rather than duplicating one rule.
Policy-registry and request/child source-order variants must serialize to the same public PolicyDecision.
Permission-denied evidence is capability-based: assert fail-closed behavior when PermissionDenied is reproducible, otherwise return after restoring permissions on privileged runners.
The historical S5, S5.1, and S5.2 session records remain unchanged; this follow-up record preserves the original evidence and documents its correction.
handoff: E09.S6 must accept only AuthorizedExecutionPlan and preserve the corrected S5.2 evidence. Before treating this follow-up as a pinned-gate closure, rerun repository-pinned format and Clippy/Moon gates or confirm equivalent CI.
blockers: Follow-up delta still needs repository-pinned Rust 1.96.0 / Moon 2.3.2 format and Clippy confirmation; those tools were unavailable in this environment.

ontarch:secrets-scan detect reports a pre-existing age-secret-key in git history (commit a3a4978, 2026-06-24; redacted in HEAD, persists in blob) — not introduced by this correction; human decision pending.
token_note: STATE + S5.2 validation follow-up + S5.2 addendum + last 2 session records vs full E09/Plan
